english: First Bowl Rice Kitchen sharing set

food_subgroup: Sharing set meal comprising rice bowls, proteins, and side dishes for group dining

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-component Chinese sharing bundle set. FOODON_00002473 (meal food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (composite food - mixed dishes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a Chinese sharing bundle set meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a multi-component Chinese sharing bundle set meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One sharing bundle set intended for four persons, estimated at approximately 1600 g total (400 g per person), comprising rice, protein dishes, and vegetable sides.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from HPB Singapore Foodo and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: steamed white rice, braised chicken, stir-fried vegetables, soy-based sauces
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a 4-pax Chinese sharing bundle set. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition of typical components: steamed white rice (~50% by weight), braised/stir-fried chicken (~25%), mixed vegetables (~15%), and soy-based sauces/condiments (~10%), cross-referenced against HPB Foodo entries for Chinese rice bowl dishes and USDA SR Legacy entries for individual ingredients. Values represent a plausible per-100g average for the combined set.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — steamed white rice and mixed Chinese rice-based meal analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a 4-pax Chinese sharing bundle set. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate component being steamed white rice (GI ~64–72 per University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the presence of protein (chicken), fat, and dietary fibre from vegetables, which lower the effective meal GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI 65 × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 78.4 g for 400 g serving at 19.6 g carb/100g) / 100 ≈ 51.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg/100g; ~1520 mg per 400 g serving) from soy sauce and braising liquids
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-sodium soy sauce preparations, as a single 400 g serving may contribute approximately 1520 mg sodium, representing over 65% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~65) driven by steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor total carbohydrate intake per serving (approximately 78 g per 400 g portion) and consider substituting white rice with brown rice or reducing rice portion to manage postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~1.1 g/100g) and elevated sodium (~380 mg/100g) from soy-based sauces and animal proteins
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the combined sodium and saturated fat load in this sharing set and favour vegetable-heavy portions while limiting sauce consumption to reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 4 Pax Sharing Bundle Set is a Chinese-cuisine group meal offered by First Bowl Rice Kitchen at Funan, Singapore, designed for four diners sharing together. The set typically comprises steamed white rice, one or more braised or stir-fried protein dishes (commonly chicken, pork, or tofu), and a selection of stir-fried or blanched vegetables, all seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and aromatics such as ginger and spring onion. The meal reflects the Singaporean Chinese rice kitchen tradition of communal dining, where multiple dishes are placed at the centre of the table and shared family-style. Texturally, the set offers soft steamed rice, tender braised proteins, and lightly cooked vegetables with savoury umami-forward saucing. Nutritionally, the set provides a balanced macronutrient profile per 100 g (approximately 148 kcal, 8.5 g protein, 4.2 g fat, 19.5 g carbohydrate), though the sodium content is elevated due to soy-based sauces and is a key concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), driven primarily by steamed white rice, and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor total carbohydrate intake per serving. The set is priced at SGD 42.00 and is available on GrabFood Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific commercial sharing bundle set. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition of typical Chinese rice kitchen components cross-referenced against HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy. The exact composition of the First Bowl Rice Kitchen 4 Pax Sharing Bundle Set is not publicly disclosed in any approved nutritional database. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy sources used for this composite estimate. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this composite estimate. All other -1 values have been replaced with estimated numeric values where defensible component data was available. GI is an analogous estimate based on steamed white rice as the dominant carbohydrate source, moderated by protein and fat co-ingestion; gi_category is medium per University of Sydney GI Database analogous evidence. Sodium content is notably elevated due to soy-based sauces typical of Chinese rice kitchen preparations. The halal status is listed as false as First Bowl Rice Kitchen - Funan is not confirmed halal-certified in publicly available records at time of processing; this should be verified. Serving size of 1600 g (400 g per person × 4) is an estimate based on typical Chinese sharing set meal portions. The aliases_by_language hindi entry '4 लोगों का शेयरिंग बंडल सेट' uses the Hindi transliteration of 'sharing bundle set' and is consistent with the flat aliases list.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient profile from HPB Foodo and USDA SR Legacy component data. Added all mandatory multilingual names for Singaporean Chinese cuisine. Populated ontology with closest_parent codes and gap notes. Added GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, selenium_mcg) in notes. Confirmed hindi alias in flat aliases list matches aliases_by_language entry exactly.

Iteration 2: Corrected a character inconsistency in the Hindi alias between 'aliases_by_language' and 'aliases' (changed 'शेययरिंग' to 'शेयरिंग' in aliases_by_language.hindi to match aliases). Removed a duplicate entry for '四人份套餐' from the flat aliases list. Re-verified full consistency between aliases_by_language entries and the flat aliases list.
